channel.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: politics
channel.description: Get the latest news online at Major Digest, the top online news resource for the most reliable news sources. Stay informed about the world around you!

image.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: politics

item.title: In southwest Minnesota, cities take different approaches to recreational cannabis market
item.description: The Minnesota Legislature set most of the rules about cultivation and sale of recreational marijuana in spring 2023. However, the state left several key decisions within local control, including whether cities can allow more than a set minimum of shops.
item.dc:creator: Hannah Yang, MPR News

item.title: Top GOP rebel angles for powerful House leadership-backed committee post
item.description: Rep. Chip Roy is floating himself as a possible candidate to chair the influential House Rules Committee next year.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: White House releases national strategy to combat Islamophobia
item.description: WASHINGTON (AP) — The White House on Thursday announced what it called the first-ever national strategy to counter Islamophobia, detailing more than 100 steps federal officials are taking to curb hate, violence, bias and discrimination against Muslims and Arab Americans.
item.dc:creator: WILL WEISSERT, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Biden administration launches national strategy to combat Islamophobia, anti-Arab hate
item.description: The Biden administration has launched a national strategy to combat Islamophobia and anti-Muslim hate. The initiative comes a year after Hamas' attack on Israel.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Trump offers support for dockworkers union by saying ports shouldn't install more automated systems
item.description: WASHINGTON (AP) — President-elect Donald Trump on Thursday voiced his support for the dockworkers union before their contract expires next month at Eastern and Gulf Coast ports, saying that any further “automation” of the ports would harm workers.
item.dc:creator: JOSH BOAK, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Biden's full list of clemency and commutation recipients revealed
item.description: The White House on Thursday unveiled a list of names of the 39 individuals whom President Biden pardoned, as well as a list of the nearly 1,500 individuals whose sentences he commuted.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: NYC Mayor Adams meets with Trump border czar: 'We have the same desire' to go after illegal migrant criminals
item.description: New York City Mayor Eric Adams and incoming border czar Tom Homan met Thursday to talk about potential cooperation between local and federal authorities.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: RFK Jr.'s daughter-in-law met with Ratcliffe about CIA job: Washington Post
item.description: Robert F. Kennedy Jr.’s daughter-in-law took a private meeting with John Ratcliffe, President-elect Trump’s pick to run the CIA, in an apparent bid for a high-ranking position within the agency, The Washington Post reported.
item.dc:creator: Hanna Trudo, Fox 5

item.title: US Senate chaplain Barry Black hospitalized after brain bleed
item.description: WASHINGTON (AP) — U.S. Senate Chaplain Barry Black has been hospitalized after suffering a bleed on his brain and is expected to have a “smooth recovery,” his office said.
item.dc:creator: MARY CLARE JALONICK, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Trump invites China's Xi to next month's inauguration
item.description: President-elect Donald Trump is breaking with precedent in inviting a foreign head of state to his inauguration.
item.dc:creator: Deepa Shivaram, MPR News

item.title: Military service academies see drop in reported sexual assaults after alarming surge
item.description: WASHINGTON (AP) — Reported sexual assaults at the U.S. military service academies dropped in 2024 for the second year in a row, according to new Pentagon data, marking a sharp turnaround from an alarming surge two years ago that triggered sweeping reviews and an overhaul in leadership.
item.dc:creator: LOLITA C. BALDOR, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Khanna eyes Defense Department contracting, excess property for DOGE action
item.description: "I want the U.S. to have the greatest military in the world and the resources to counter increasingly sophisticated threats from our adversaries, but we need a more sensible approach," Khanna wrote.
item.dc:creator: Juliann Ventura, NewsNation

item.title: FBI not involved in Jan. 6 riots: Report
item.description: The FBI reportedly failed to properly canvass field offices for threat information ahead of the riots.
item.dc:creator: Steph Whiteside, NewsNation

item.title: China denies new report linking CCP to four sites in Cuba allegedly used to spy on the US
item.description: The Center for Strategic and International Studies released a report last week detailing facilities in Cuba that it claims China may be using to gather signal intelligence on the U.S.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: McConnell working from home after tripping at Capitol
item.description: Senate Republican Leader Mitch McConnell (Ky.) missed his usual morning speech to open the Senate on Thursday after falling earlier in the week and suffering a minor cut to the face and a sprained wrist.
item.dc:creator: Alexander Bolton, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: FAA administrator to resign on Trump swearing-in
item.description: Mike Whitaker is leaving at the start of President-elect Trump’s term, a source familiar with his plans told The Hill.
item.dc:creator: Alex Gangitano, NewsNation

item.title: Ontario to restrict electricity exports to U.S. and bar American-made alcohol if Trump tariffs applied
item.description: Ontario may considering barring American-made alcohol in addition to restricting electricity exports to Minnesota, Michigan and New York state.
item.dc:creator: The Associated Press, MPR News

item.title: What Americans think about Hegseth, Gabbard and key Trump Cabinet picks: AP-NORC poll
item.description: As several of President-elect Donald Trump’s choices for high-level positions in his administration face scrutiny on Capitol Hill, a poll from The Associated Press-NORC Center for Public Affairs Research shows that Americans have their own doubts.
item.dc:creator: Linley Sanders And Meg Kinnard, Associated Press, KPRC 2

item.title: Biden commutes roughly 1,500 sentences and pardons 39 people in biggest single-day act of clemency
item.description: President Joe Biden is commuting the sentences of roughly 1,500 people who were released from prison and placed on home confinement during the coronavirus pandemic and pardoning 39 Americans convicted of nonviolent crimes.
item.dc:creator: Colleen Long, Associated Press, KPRC 2

item.title: Teacher sues Beverly Hills school district for racism after Trump win
item.description: The lawyer told NewsNation there were several specific incidents, including a video showing students mocking Black students in the school cafeteria.
item.dc:creator: Damita Menezes, NewsNation

item.title: Lombardo joins GOP governors 'fully committed' to Trump deportation threats
item.description: Gov. Joe Lombardo and 25 other Republican governors lined up in support of President-elect Donald Trump's immigration policies on Wednesday, calling for the deportation of "individuals who pose a threat.
item.dc:creator: Greg Haas, KLAS, CBS News

item.title: Kimberly Guilfoyle is the latest Trump family ally chosen for an ambassadorship
item.description: Kimberly Guilfoyle, who got engaged to Trump's son in 2020, is the latest appointee he's chosen from his family tree. There's a long and controversial history of presidents picking relatives for jobs.
item.dc:creator: Rachel Treisman, MPR News

item.title: Hegseth makes play to win hard-to-get GOP centrists
item.description: Pete Hegseth is looking to win over a pair of key Republican moderates as he tries to keep his hopes of leading the Defense Department from being derailed. The former Fox News host huddled with Sen. Lisa Murkowski (R-Alaska) on Tuesday and is set to do the same on Wednesday with Sen.
item.dc:creator: Al Weaver, NewsNation

item.title: Florida State Rep. Susan Valdes, representing parts of Hillsborough County, switches political parties
item.description: State Rep. Susan Valdes, who represents Florida's 64th District, including parts of Hillsborough County, switched political parties, the Florida House of Representatives announced Monday.
item.dc:creator: Sara Filips, WFLA, NBC

item.title: Mayoral hopeful Michael Blake discusses vision for NYC
item.description: NEW YORK (PIX11) – As we approach 2025, the new year marks a new election cycle. Political consultant Michael Blake joins PIX on Politics to discuss his decision to join the long list of Democrats competing with Mayor Eric Adams in next year’s mayoral primary.
item.dc:creator: Dan Mannarino, PIX 11
